1.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
1.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

1.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 650 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
1.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

1.name: Sweet-Spot Run — 3 mi at moderate effort
1.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at sustained moderate effort (~RPE 6, conversational in short sentences — meaningfully harder than your easy runs but not threshold). Cool down 1.5 mi easy. Builds aerobic durability without the recovery cost of intervals.

1.name: Swim Threshold — 800 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
1.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

1.name: Tri Performance — Base Hypertrophy (Lower)
1.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Base Week 1 — Two primary lower compounds (65% 1RM, RIR 3) plus single-leg RDL 2×8/leg and Hip Thrusts 3×8-10 for glute drive; accessories stay light. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells.

strength_exercises.name: DB Romanian Deadlift
strength_exercises.notes: Hip hinge — bike + run power base

strength_exercises.name: Goblet Squat
strength_exercises.notes: Knee drive — single DB held at chest

strength_exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL
strength_exercises.notes: Unilateral hip hinge — better running transfer than bilateral; 2s lowering

strength_exercises.name: Glute Bridges
strength_exercises.weight: Bodyweight — full hip extension at top
strength_exercises.notes: No bench — floor glute bridges; reps bumped to maintain stimulus

strength_exercises.name: Calf Raises (Bilateral)
strength_exercises.notes: 3s lower — Achilles resilience

1.name: Long Ride — 2.0 hr
1.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

1.name: Long Run — 8.5 mi
1.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

2.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
2.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

2.name: Tri Performance — Hypertrophy Deload (Upper)
2.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Hypertrophy Deload Week 2 — reduced volume to preserve strength stimulus while supporting total recovery. 2 sets per lift @ 62% 1RM, RIR 4+. No accessories. Equipment — Required: Bench, Dumbbells.

2.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1200 yd
2.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing); 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery). 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

8.name: Long Run — 5 mi
8.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

9.name: Bike Threshold — 2×20 min
9.description: Warm up 15 min with fast-pedal spins. 2×20 min at FTP (Zone 4 — hard but sustainable). 5 min easy between. Cool down 10 min.

9.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 1100 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (11 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

9.name: Endurance Swim — 1200 yd
9.description: Warm up 400 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). Main set — 2×250 yd easy aerobic with 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd.

9.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
9.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

9.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
9.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

9.name: Swim Threshold — 1100 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

9.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
9.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

9.name: Tri Performance — Strength Build (Lower)
9.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Build Week 9 — Two heavy compounds (3 working sets each, 80% 1RM, RIR 2) plus Hip Thrusts 3×8 for glute drive. Hamstring Nordics live in base; build stays squat + hinge volume-capped. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells.

strength_exercises.name: Glute Bridges
strength_exercises.weight: Bodyweight — full hip extension at top
strength_exercises.notes: No bench — floor glute bridges; reps bumped to maintain stimulus

9.name: Brick — Bike 2.0 hr

9.name: Brick — Run 3.5 mi off the bike
9.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

11.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
11.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

11.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
11.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

11.name: Swim Threshold — 1100 yd
11.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

11.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
11.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

11.name: Tri Performance — Strength Build (Lower)
11.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Build Week 11 — Two heavy compounds (4 working sets each, 85% 1RM, RIR 2) plus Hip Thrusts 3×8 for glute drive. Hamstring Nordics live in base; build stays squat + hinge volume-capped. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells.

strength_exercises.name: Glute Bridges
strength_exercises.weight: Bodyweight — full hip extension at top
strength_exercises.notes: No bench — floor glute bridges; reps bumped to maintain stimulus

strength_exercises.name: Weighted Single-Leg Calf Raises
strength_exercises.notes: 3s eccentric

11.name: Brick — Bike 2.3 hr
11.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

11.name: Brick — Run 4 mi off the bike
11.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

11.name: Long Run — 11 mi
11.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

12.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
12.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

12.name: Tri Performance — Hypertrophy Deload (Upper)
12.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Hypertrophy Deload Week 12 — reduced volume to preserve strength stimulus while supporting total recovery. 2 sets per lift @ 62% 1RM, RIR 4+. No accessories. Equipment — Required: Bench, Dumbbells.

12.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1350 yd
12.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery); 4×50 Fist Swim (early vertical forearm). 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

12.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
12.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

12.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
12.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

12.name: Recovery Swim — 900 yd
12.description: Warm up 200 yd easy. 5×100 yd at easy aerobic effort (20 sec rest). Cool down 200 yd.

12.name: Easy Run — 2 mi
12.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

12.name: Tri Performance — Hypertrophy Deload (Lower)

Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Hypertrophy Deload Week 12 — reduced volume to preserve strength stimulus while supporting total recovery. 2 sets per lift @ 62% 1RM, RIR 4+. No accessories. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells.

12.name: Long Ride — 1.3 hr
12.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

12.name: Long Run — 5 mi
12.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

13.name: Bike VO2max — 3×5 min
13.description: Warm up 15 min. 3×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

13.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (10 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

13.name: Endurance Swim — 900 yd
13.description: Warm up 400 yd easy. Main set — 2×150 yd easy aerobic with 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd.

13.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
13.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

13.name: Half-Marathon Pace Run — 3 mi
13.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 3 mi at 70.3 / half-marathon run pace (Z3–Z4), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

13.name: Swim Threshold — 900 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

13.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
13.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

13.name: Tri Performance — Maintenance + Power (Lower)
13.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Maintenance + Power Week 13 — Power-focused: 3-5 reps lift @ 70% 1RM, RIR 2, paired with plyo. Express strength as power; minimal fatigue. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells.

16.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
16.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

16.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
16.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr

16.name: Easy Run — 4 mi
16.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

16.name: Swim Threshold — 800 yd
16.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Tri Performance — Taper Priming
16.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Race week — approximately 3-4 days before race day, ~25 min. 1 lower compound + 1 upper compound at 50-60% 1RM for 2×3-4 fast reps, plus 2 light-band activation accessories. No plyometrics. Skip-optional if you feel sharp. Goal is neural drive, not fatigue. Equipment — Required: Bench, Dumbbells, Bands.

17.name: Race-Week Activation Swim — 800 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×50 yd build accelerations (start easy, finish strong; long rest between — pure neuromuscular sharpener, NOT a hard interval). 2×100 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Tri Performance — Taper Priming
17.description: Loads will be conservative (bodyweight-based defaults: squat 1.0×BW, deadlift 1.25×BW, bench 0.75×BW, OHP 0.5×BW) until you complete a baseline test or enter your 1RM. Log a 1RM in your profile to unlock progressive loading. Race week — approximately 3-4 days before race day, ~25 min. 1 lower compound + 1 upper compound at 50-60% 1RM for 2×3-4 fast reps, plus 2 light-band activation accessories. No plyometrics. Skip-optional if you feel sharp. Goal is neural drive, not fatigue. Equipment — Required: Bench, Dumbbells, Bands.

17.name: Test 70.3 race
17.description: Race day. Swim 1.2mi → Bike 56mi → Run 13.1mi. No add-on training; execute pacing and fueling.

9: Week 9 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (2h vs 2.25h). Lengthening toward 2.25h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
11: Week 11 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (2.25h vs 2.5h). Lengthening toward 2.5h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
13: Week 13 has no long run scheduled — race-specific plans target a 11+ mi long run for durability.
